artifact: P226 baseline reproduction plan and baseline selection
claim_boundary: This artifact selects a reproducible baseline route and records a bounded local smoke reproduction. It does not claim a full benchmark improvement or new network result yet.
task_route: Find local baselines and code, reproduce a minimal baseline, then propose a small module replacement/addition suitable for a paper story.

do_not_use_as_main_baseline.reason: P193 has proxy-leakage risk and P195 remains blocked without independent labels.

do_not_use_as_main_baseline.baseline: P155/P176 admission baseline as main story
do_not_use_as_main_baseline.reason: It is useful context, but B1 statistics conflict across artifacts: tools/run_baseline_comparison.py currently reports B1 admitted=19, while paper/evidence/baseline_statistics_p176.json reports B1 admitted=9 from supplement-derived per-cluster flags.

baseline_candidates.name: DROID-SLAM raw vs semantic/dynamic masked frontend
baseline_candidates.fair_comparison: Same sequence, same frame window, same DROID weights/runtime, raw RGB vs masked RGB only changes frontend masking.
baseline_candidates.strengths: Most directly supports dynamic-SLAM paper story; already has 64-frame multi-session evidence and ORB cross-check context.
baseline_candidates.risks: Sparse or selective masks are often trajectory-neutral, so improvement may require a targeted dynamic-object sequence or frontend feature-retention metric rather than only APE/RPE.

baseline_candidates.name: ORB-SLAM3 raw vs masked cross-check
baseline_candidates.fair_comparison: Same mono TUM path and raw/masked images, but sparse tracking coverage weakens statistical power.
baseline_candidates.risks: Monocular scale ambiguity and short tracking coverage make it a secondary cross-check, not the main baseline.

baseline_candidates.name: P218 compact binary dynamic-mask segmentation baseline
baseline_candidates.fair_comparison: Same P217 dataset-mask-supervised split and same leakage guard; architecture variants can be compared directly.
baseline_candidates.strengths: Clear network baseline with room for temporal/boundary/motion-aware module additions.
baseline_candidates.risks: Default training script writes fixed P218 evidence paths, so P226/P227 variants should first add output override support or a separate variant script to avoid overwriting baseline evidence.

baseline_candidates.name: P155/P176 admission-policy baselines B0/B1/B2
baseline_candidates.fair_comparison: Cluster-level paired policies, but not the requested learned dynamic-mask or SLAM frontend story.
baseline_candidates.strengths: Good supporting motivation for rejecting dynamic agents.
baseline_candidates.risks: B1 mismatch and proxy-leakage concerns make it unsuitable as the main baseline.

reproduced_experiment.name: p226_repro8
reproduced_experiment.baseline: DROID-SLAM raw vs masked frontend smoke
commands: /home/rui/miniconda3/envs/tram/bin/python tools/evaluate_dynamic_slam_metrics.py --input-dir outputs/dynamic_slam_backend_input_pack_p226_repro8 --output-dir outputs/dynamic_slam_backend_smoke_p226_repro8 --artifact "P226 reproduced 8-frame DROID-SLAM raw-vs-masked smoke metrics" --scope "P226 bounded local reproduction: 8 frames, Jun. 23 2022 Aisle_CW_Run_1, repository example forklift mask on frame 000002 only." --masked-label "masked RGB (example forklift frame 000002)" --output-prefix p226_repro8_metrics --interpretation "Both raw and masked 8-frame DROID-SLAM runs completed locally. This is a smoke reproduction, not the main 64-frame benchmark; the mask is sparse and expected to be trajectory-neutral."
reproduced_experiment.interpretation: The minimal baseline is locally reproducible and trajectory-neutral at the expected scale for a sparse one-frame example mask. This verifies the raw-vs-masked DROID pipeline before adding a learned or temporal frontend module.

recommended_main_baseline.primary: DROID-SLAM raw vs masked temporal/dynamic frontend
recommended_main_baseline.rationale: Use DROID raw-vs-masked as the paper-level SLAM baseline because it has local reproducibility, backend metrics, and a direct dynamic-SLAM story. Use P218 compact UNet as the module baseline to improve mask quality before feeding learned masks into the same DROID/ORB evaluation path.

module_replacement_plan.module: confidence-gated mask dilation for SLAM frontend
module_replacement_plan.implementation: Add mask post-processing modes: confidence/coverage gate, small-hole close, boundary dilation cap, and per-frame coverage ceiling. Preserve raw/masked pairing and write policy fields to backend_input_manifest.json.
module_replacement_plan.expected_metric: Maintain APE/RPE within baseline tolerance while improving ORB/DROID frontend dynamic feature suppression; success threshold: no more than 1 mm APE RMSE degradation on low-coverage sessions and fewer features inside dynamic masks.
module_replacement_plan.risk: Over-dilation removes static texture and hurts tracking.
module_replacement_plan.proof: Run same 8/64-frame raw-vs-masked pack with dilation sweep and compare APE/RPE plus mask coverage.

files_to_change: tools/build_temporal_masked_sequence_p225.py or a new P227 temporal training script
module_replacement_plan.implementation: Extend SmallUNet with an auxiliary temporal consistency loss over adjacent frames or add a light recurrent/previous-mask input head when P225 sequence pack is ready.
module_replacement_plan.expected_metric: Improve validation/test dynamic IoU or F1 over P218 baseline without reducing recall; target +1 to +3 IoU points in bounded smoke.
module_replacement_plan.risk: P225 temporal pack may not align masks perfectly; temporal smoothing can erase true object boundaries.
module_replacement_plan.proof: Use P217/P225 split-safe sequences, compare against frozen P218 config and all-background/all-dynamic baselines.

module_replacement_plan.implementation: Add an auxiliary boundary target derived from mask edges and a small boundary logits head sharing the UNet decoder.
module_replacement_plan.expected_metric: Better boundary F1 and fewer foreground leaks into static texture; dynamic IoU should stay at least P218 baseline or slightly lower while SLAM masking becomes cleaner.
module_replacement_plan.risk: Extra loss can overfit the small 237-row mask dataset.
module_replacement_plan.proof: Report dynamic IoU/F1 plus boundary F1; then feed predicted masks to DROID frontend smoke.

module_replacement_plan.module: learned mask gating in SLAM frontend
files_to_change: new tool for exporting P218 predictions into backend input packs
module_replacement_plan.implementation: Use P218 predicted probabilities as soft gates: mask only pixels above a selected threshold and cap per-frame coverage.
module_replacement_plan.expected_metric: DROID/ORB trajectory remains at baseline or slightly below while dynamic-agent feature contamination drops versus raw.
module_replacement_plan.risk: Predicted masks from still images may not transfer to sequence frames without temporal adaptation.
module_replacement_plan.proof: Same raw-vs-mask pack, same backend, predicted-mask vs dataset-mask vs raw comparison.

next_actions: Do not use P193/P195 weak-label admission as a learned baseline.
Wait for or inspect P225 temporal sequence package before implementing temporal loss.
First small safe implementation should be output-path overrides for P218 variant evidence, then a boundary or confidence-gated post-processing module.
Run 8-frame smoke first, then 64-frame DROID session(s), then ORB-SLAM3 as secondary cross-check.
